chapter_title.en: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations

text: 4320 - وَ قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِيمَنِ اِسْتَحْلَفَ رَجُلاً مِنْ أَهْلِ اَلْكِتَابِ بِيَمِينِ صَبْرٍ أَنْ يَسْتَحْلِفَهُ بِكِتَابِهِ وَ مِلَّتِهِ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4320 - Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled concerning a case where someone required a man from the People of the Book to take a solemn oath.
Imam (as) said that the oath should be taken according to his scripture and his religion.
